title: Method of determining toxicity of an immunomodulatory drug
abstract: Humanized mouse models and methods are provided for determining whether administration of an immunomodulatory drug likely elicits a severe cytokine release syndrome in a human. Humanized mouse models and methods are also provided for determining the immunotoxicity in a human of a drug candidate or of drug combinations.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method, comprising: measuring at least one cytokine level in a blood sample from an irradiated NSG-SGM3 or NOG mouse that is engrafted with human peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs), and has been administered a drug candidate, wherein the at least one cytokine is selected from the group consisting of IFN-g, IL-10, IL-6, IL-2, IL-4, and TNFa, and the at least one cytokine level is indicative of severity of immunotoxicity of the drug candidate. 2. A method of assessing immunotoxicity of a drug candidate, the method comprising: administering a drug candidate to an irradiated NSG-SGM3 or NOG mouse that is engrafted with human peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs); measuring at least one cytokine level in a blood sample from the mouse, wherein the at least one cytokine is selected from the group consisting of IFN-g, IL-10, IL-6, IL-2, IL-4, and TNFa; and assessing immunotoxicity of the drug candidate, wherein the at least one cytokine level is indicative of severity of immunotoxicity of the drug candidate. 3. A method, comprising: measuring at least one cytokine level in a blood sample from an irradiated immunodeficient mouse selected from the group consisting of a NSG-derivative mouse, a NOG- derivative mouse, a NRG or NRG-derivative mouse, a NCG or NCG-derivative mouse, wherein the immunodeficient mouse is engrafted with human peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs), and has been administered a drug candidate, wherein the at least one cytokine is selected from the group consisting of IFN-g, IL-10, IL-6, IL-2, IL-4, and TNFa, and the at least one cytokine level is indicative of severity of immunotoxicity of the drug candidate, optionally wherein the immunodeficient mouse is not a NSG-CSF-l mouse or an NSG-IL-6 mouse. 4. A method of assessing immunotoxicity of a drug candidate, the method comprising: administering a drug candidate to an irradiated immunodeficient mouse selected from the group consisting of a NSG-derivative mouse, a NOG-derivative mouse, a NRG or NRG- derivative mouse, a NCG or NCG-derivative mouse, wherein the immunodeficient mouse is engrafted with human peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs), optionally wherein the immunodeficient mouse is not a SG-CSF-l mouse or an NSG-IL-6 mouse; 

 measuring at least one cytokine level in a blood sample obtained from the mouse, wherein the at least one cytokine is selected from the group consisting of IFN-g, IL-10, IL-6, IL- 2, IL-4, and TNFa; and assessing immunotoxicity of the drug candidate, wherein the at least one cytokine level is indicative of severity of immunotoxicity of the drug candidate. 5. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mouse is engrafted with 1 x 10 7 - 5 x 10 7 human PBMCs. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the mouse is engrafted with 1.5 x 10 7 - 3 x 10 7 human PBMCs. 7. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the drug candidate is administered to the mouse 5 - 7 days following engraftment with the PBMCs. 8. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mouse is irradiated with a 75 - 125 centigray (cGy) dose of X-ray radiation. 9. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one cytokine comprises IFN-g. 10. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one cytokine comprises IL-10. 11. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one cytokine comprises IL-6. 12. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one cytokine comprises IL-2. 13. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one cytokine comprises IL-4. 14. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one cytokine comprises TNFa. 15. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the at least one cytokine level is measured 2 - 6 hours following administration of the drug candidate. 16. The method of any one of the preceding claims further comprising determining that the severity of immunotoxicity of the drug candidate is high when: an IFN-g level in the mouse is > 1,800 pg/ml Â±10%; an IL-10 level in the mouse is > 120 pg/ml Â±10%; an IL-6 level in the mouse is > 25 pg/ml Â±10%; an IL-2 level in the mouse is > 80 pg/ml Â±10%; an IL-4 level in the mouse is > 120 pg/ml Â±10%; and/or a TNFa level in the mouse is > 120 pg/ml Â±10%. 17. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the drug candidate is an immuno stimulating drug. 18. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the drug candidate is a checkpoint inhibitor. 19. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the drug candidate is an interferon. 20. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the drug candidate is a monoclonal antibody. 21. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the drug candidate is a bispecific antibody. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the bispecific antibody is a bi-specific T cell engager (BiTEÂ®). 23. The method of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the drug candidate is a small molecule drug.
